why: the single most overused AI/SaaS look; reads as templated
do_instead: pick a subject-specific dominant + one sharp accent; atmosphere via subtle grain/gradient in the brand hue, not blurple

banned_default: Inter / Roboto / system-ui as the only typeface
why: the default "safe" face; zero personality, no display/body contrast
do_instead: pick a characterful display + a distinct body (see type-pairings.csv); keep ≥3x size + heavy weight contrast
if the brief wants warm/editorial, shift ground off cream (bone/paper-cool), use a cool or oxblood accent, and an unexpected display face
only if the subject truly is cyber; then break it with an unusual layout grammar and a second supporting hue

banned_default: broadsheet hairline-rule dense columns, no rhythm
why: the "newspaper" slop cluster; flat and airless
do_instead: introduce asymmetry, a signature element, and atmosphere; let whitespace and one accent carry hierarchy

banned_default: 🚀✨🎯 emoji standing in for an icon set
do_instead: use a real icon set (inline SVG); zero emoji in nav/buttons/feature bullets/empty states

banned_default: centered hero + 3 equal feature cards + accordion FAQ + CTA band, all same padded box
why: the predictable AI layout; no rhythm or asymmetry
do_instead: vary section width/rhythm; one signature layout element; break the equal-cards grid
give the ground atmosphere (subtle gradient/grain/pattern) and pick a neutral with a slight hue bias toward the accent
